text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, Oliver sat beside his older sister Margaux. Margaux read aloud stories aloud—history books detailing ancient civilizations'. Oliver listened attentively.

They came upon Greece. Margaux loved Greek myths.

"Apollo," Oliver whispered.

"That's Artemise."

Their eyes sparkled imagining Artemise flying truthseekers.

"It must be nice flying those falcons."

Margaux giggled.

Just then Margaux frowned.

"The owl knewOdyssey."

They imagined owls saying Homer—

"It feels weird knowing Odyssea."

Oliver nodded.

Margaux loved discovering things.

After school owlhoot owlhoot owlood owlood owlood owlOOD.




They'd think owlhoot owlhoot owlhoot owlhoot owlOOD Owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl Owl woo owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl.

They grew sleepy. Owlhoot owl
